Home / Glossary / ETL vs ELT
Glossary

What is the difference between ETL and ELT?

Updated 2026-04-15

ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) cleans and reshapes data on a separate server before loading it into a warehouse, which suits regulated industries with on-premise constraints. ELT (Extract, Load, Transform) loads raw data first and uses the warehouse's compute (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks) to transform it — faster, cheaper at scale, and the modern default.

~$3 per TB
Approximate Snowflake credit cost of an ELT transform pass on warehouse-native compute, often cheaper than running a dedicated ETL fleet.
Snowflake pricing (Standard edition, 2024)
78%
of new data-warehouse projects in 2023 chose an ELT-first architecture per dbt Labs' State of Analytics Engineering.
dbt Labs — State of Analytics Engineering 2023

Side-by-side comparison

AspectETLELT
Where transforms runDedicated ETL serverInside the warehouse
Storage shapeOnly modeled data landsRaw + modeled both land
ReprocessingRe-extract from sourceRe-run SQL on raw layer
ToolingInformatica, Talend, SSISdbt, Dataform, SQLMesh
Best forRegulated, on-prem, slow source systemsCloud warehouses, agile teams

Why the world moved to ELT

Three things flipped the default: (1) cloud warehouses made compute cheap and elastic, (2) keeping raw data became a competitive advantage for retroactive analysis, and (3) dbt made transform-as-SQL the lingua franca of analytics engineering.

Frequently asked questions

Is ELT always cheaper?

No. For tiny datasets and infrequent runs, a small ETL server is cheaper than warm warehouse compute. ELT wins at scale and on bursty workloads.

Can I mix ETL and ELT?

Yes — many teams ETL the lightweight transforms (PII masking, deduping) and ELT the heavyweight ones (joins, aggregations). It's called ETLT.

Where does DataVision Pro fit?

DataVision Pro is downstream of both — it consumes a clean CSV/Excel extract and runs the analysis layer, freeing your warehouse from BI workloads.

Is reverse ETL the opposite?

Not the opposite — reverse ETL pushes warehouse data back into operational tools (CRM, ad platforms). It complements ELT rather than replacing it.

Which is better for compliance?

ETL historically wins because sensitive fields can be masked before they ever land in the warehouse. ELT can match it with column-level encryption + masking policies.

Related terms

Data Cleaning

The process of detecting and correcting errors, inconsistencies, missing values, and duplicates in a dataset before analysis.

Descriptive Statistics

Summary measures (mean, median, std, percentiles) that describe the central tendency and spread of a dataset.

Predictive Analytics

Using historical data and statistical or ML models to forecast future outcomes or behaviours.

Data Drift

A change over time in the statistical properties of input data that degrades a model's accuracy.
